response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a versatile full-stack and systems developer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and testing-culture credentials, but he falls critically short of the core requirements for this role. The job demands 8 years of C++ experience as its primary technical bar — Bogdan's C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project, totaling well under 2 years. Equally, the role requires 5 years building large-scale infrastructure or cloud compute systems; while he has touched AWS CDK, Kubernetes, and high-throughput services at Sociomantic, none of this constitutes owning or leading an internal cloud compute platform. The preferred cloud-architecture leadership and mentoring of DevOps/cloud engineers is also largely absent.
strengths: Proven software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) and track record of sharing knowledge through workshops and community talks.
Exposure to high-throughput distributed micro-services at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O), demonstrating some distributed-systems awareness.
Staff-level breadth across frontend, backend, embedded, and DevOps tooling, indicating ability to navigate cross-cutting technical concerns.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months professionally) against a mandatory 8-year requirement — the single largest disqualifier for this role.
No demonstrated ownership or technical leadership of a cloud compute platform or equivalent large-scale infrastructure initiative.
No evidence of mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers, formal technical-leadership scope, or managing platform-level architectural decisions at the scale this role requires.
